## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On February 23, 1945, during the massive, incredibly brutal Battle of Iwo Jima, Joe Rosenthal captured the absolute, most iconic photograph of World War II: "Raising the Flag on Iwo Jima." The massive, incredibly powerful image of six United States Marines heavily struggling to raise the American flag atop Mount Suribachi became an absolute, massive symbol of American military resolve and was heavily utilized to drive massive, multi-million dollar war bond drives back home.

On February 23, 1997, scientists at the Roslin Institute in Scotland officially and publicly announced the massive, historic cloning of Dolly the sheep. As the absolute, very first mammal to be successfully cloned from an adult somatic cell, the massive announcement shocked the global scientific community. The breakthrough instantly triggered a massive, highly polarized global debate regarding the bioethics, legality, and terrifying, massive potential of human cloning.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On February 23, 1905, Paul Harris officially and formally founded the very first massive Rotary Club in Chicago, Illinois. While originating in the United States, the massive, international service organization rapidly expanded globally. The Rotary Club of Calcutta (Kolkata) became the first massive branch established in India in 1919, fundamentally introducing the massive, highly organized concept of secular, civic volunteerism and philanthropy to the massive Indian professional and business classes.

On February 23, 1954, massive, highly consequential linguistic and political reorganization was heavily formalized in India. The States Reorganisation Commission (SRC) was officially constituted by the central government. This massive, foundational body was tasked with aggressively redrawing the massive, complex borders of Indian states based primarily on linguistic lines, heavily attempting to accommodate massive regional aspirations and prevent the massive linguistic balkanization of the newly independent republic.
